title: GitHub Review by berendt
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 342b9d8 with Claude Code

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=1 warning=3 info=2 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## CRITICAL (1)

### C-001: Health check probes external URL when spec.gateway is set
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_healthcheck.go:57` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: keystoneStatusEndpoint(keystone) returns https://{hostname}/v3 whenever spec.gateway != nil. reconcileHealthCheck reads keystone.Status.Endpoint verbatim and performs http.Client.Do against it. After this PR the operator pod must resolve the public hostname via cluster DNS, have egress to the external VIP, and successfully traverse the entire Gateway → Service → Pod path before KeystoneAPIReady=True. This conflates ingress/DNS/cert/Gateway health with API readiness, requires Internet egress, may fail TLS verification against self-signed Gateway certs, and changes the Status.Endpoint contract for consumers.

**Code**:
```
endpoint := keystone.Status.Endpoint
resp, err := r.HTTPClient.Do(req)
```

**Action Required**: Keep Status.Endpoint as the externally advertised endpoint (the user-facing contract is good) but have reconcileHealthCheck always probe the cluster-local Service URL — e.g. introduce an unexported internalAPIURL(keystone) and have the health check use that. Add a regression test asserting the health check target is always http://{name}-api.{ns}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3 regardless of spec.gateway.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Introduce internalAPIURL(keystone) returning http://{name}-api.{ns}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3 and use it in reconcileHealthCheck instead of keystone.Status.Endpoint. Keep keystoneStatusEndpoint for the public Status.Endpoint contract.
```

---

## WARNING (3)

### W-001: Annotation removal silently ignored on HTTPRoute
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_httproute.go:221-293` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: buildKeystoneHTTPRoute only assigns route.Annotations when len(gw.Annotations) > 0. ensureHTTPRoute's merge block is gated on if route.Annotations != nil. Removing every key from spec.gateway.annotations therefore leaves the previously-applied annotations on the live HTTPRoute indefinitely — the additive merge never gets a chance to overwrite them, and there is no removal path. Gateway controllers (Kong, Istio, Envoy Gateway, Cilium) all consume implementation-specific annotations to enable/disable behavior; toggling those off via the CR will appear to silently fail. The same concern applies to labels.

**Code**:
```
if len(gw.Annotations) > 0 {
    route.Annotations = gw.Annotations
}
...
if route.Annotations != nil {
    // additive merge
}
```

**Action Required**: Apply operator-managed annotations using a known-key set (track with a keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/managed-annotations annotation listing managed keys) and remove keys not present in the desired set, OR explicitly delete the well-known operator-managed keys before re-applying. Add a test that removes a previously set annotation and asserts the live HTTPRoute no longer carries it. Apply the same fix to labels.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Track operator-managed annotation/label keys via a sentinel annotation (e.g. keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/managed-annotations) and reconcile by removing keys present in the previous set but absent from the desired set, then applying the desired set. Add a test for annotation removal.
```

---

### W-002: sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api v1.1.0 is significantly behind
**File**: `operators/keystone/go.mod:19` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: outdated dependency

**Problem**: The PR body itself acknowledges current upstream is v1.5.x. v1.1.0 (Aug 2024) predates important fixes in Accepted-condition semantics and the BackendObjectReference.Port typing tightening.

**Code**:
```
sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api v1.1.0
```

**Action Required**: Pin to the latest minor that matches the controller-runtime/k8s.io versions in use, and bump the version variable in hack/deploy-infra.sh.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Bump sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api in operators/keystone/go.mod to the latest v1.5.x release compatible with the project's controller-runtime/k8s.io versions, and update the corresponding version variable in hack/deploy-infra.sh.
```

---

### W-003: Missing Chainsaw E2E test for Gateway/HTTPRoute lifecycle
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/:1` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: E2E parity

**Problem**: 29 E2E test directories exist (network-policy, healthcheck, policy-overrides, …) but no gateway/ or httproute/ directory. Per the project pattern that introduced E2E parity for prior CC-XXXX features, a new sub-reconciler exposing a CRD field needs a Chainsaw test.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/keystone/  # no gateway/ or httproute/ directory present
```

**Action Required**: Add a Chainsaw test under tests/e2e/keystone/httproute/ that at minimum: creates a Keystone with spec.gateway, asserts the HTTPRoute is created with the right spec, asserts HTTPRouteReady flips True after a fake/real Accepted condition, and asserts the route is deleted when spec.gateway is unset.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Create tests/e2e/keystone/httproute/ with a Chainsaw scenario covering route creation, HTTPRouteReady transition, and route deletion when spec.gateway is unset.
```

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: Leftover REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT and Reviewer: please verify comments
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go:115-229` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: CC-0065 review-1 cleanup

**Problem**: Three comments are review-process scaffolding that should not survive into main: L115-120 contains a // REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT: sourcery-ai[bot] suggested ... block whose rationale is fine but the meta-framing referencing a bot review belongs in the PR thread; L182 and L229 contain leftover // ... Reviewer: please verify. (CC-0039) prompts.

**Code**:
```
// REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT: sourcery-ai[bot] suggested ...  (L115-120)
// ... Reviewer: please verify. (CC-0039)  (L182)
// ... Reviewer: please verify. (CC-0039)  (L229)
```

**Action Required**: Remove the meta-prompts to a reviewer while keeping the technical decision text intact, per the project's CC-0065 review-1 cleanup pattern.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Delete the `REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT:` framing line at L115 (keeping the technical decision rationale that follows it) and remove the trailing `Reviewer: please verify. (CC-0039)` sentences from the comments at L182 and L229.
```

---

### I-002: Partial r.Client.Get to r.Get migration in test file
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go:414-679` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: CC-0059 partial-refactor

**Problem**: Mixed style within the same file: lines 414/649/679 use the receiver shortcut r.Get(...); lines 473/547/639 still use r.Client.Get(...). This leaves a half-finished migration in the diff, contrary to the CC-0059 partial-refactor pattern.

**Code**:
```
// L414, L649, L679: r.Get(ctx, key, obj)
// L473, L547, L639: r.Client.Get(ctx, key, obj)
```

**Action Required**: Migrate all r.Client.Get(...) occurrences in this file (and ideally elsewhere in the package) to r.Get(...), or revert the new ones for consistency with the surrounding code.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
r.Get(ctx, key, obj)
```

---

## Summary

Solid plumbing for the optional HTTPRoute sub-reconciler with good pattern compliance (dual-copy CRD, RBAC sync, webhook+CEL parity, idempotency tests, RESTMapper-based graceful degradation). However, one cross-reconciler interaction was not fully thought through (health check now probes the external URL), an additive label/annotation merge has no removal path, the gateway-api dependency is significantly outdated, and the new sub-reconciler lacks a Chainsaw E2E test required by project pattern.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 1 |
| WARNING  | 3 |
| INFO     | 2 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Do not merge as-is. Block on C-1 (health check now external) and W-1 (annotation removal). Fix W-2 (gateway-api version bump) and W-3 (add E2E test) before merge, and clean up I-1 and I-2 in the same pass.
